results.name: Cyclone - Cyclone Sigma, Queensland
results.introduction: Tropical Cyclone Sigma passed Townsville to the north-east. Rain totalling 510 mm fell during the cyclone resulting in enormous seas and substantial flooding.

results.name: Cyclone - Darwin 1897
results.introduction: Late on 6 January 1897, a cyclone developed near Port Darwin, striking in the early hours of 7 January. The peak of the cyclone was recorded at approximately 3:30-4:30am. As the cyclone passed, Darwin recorded rainfall of 292 millimetres.

results.name: Cyclone - Gulf of Carpentaria, Queensland 1887
results.introduction: On 5 March 1887, a cyclone hit Burketown on the Gulf of Carpentaria. It arrived at approximately 11 am in the form of strong storm winds from the south-east. The storm continued for 12 hours; it destroyed almost the entire township and caused the death of seven from a population of just 138. The telegraph line was completely destroyed. A storm surge accompanied the cyclone, arriving at 7 pm and flooding the town.

results.name: Cyclone - Cyclone Mahina, Cape York, Queensland 1899
results.introduction: On 4 March 1899, a Category Five cyclone hit Bathurst Bay with winds reaching 260 kilometres per hour.

results.name: 2019 - Cyclone - WA - Severe tropical cyclone Veronica
results.introduction: A tropical low developed into tropical cyclone Veronica to the north-west of Broome in the early morning of Wednesday 20 March 2019 and intensified rapidly to become a severe tropical cyclone (category 4) the following day.

results.name: 2018 - Cyclone - NT - Tropical cyclone Marcus
results.introduction: On 15 March 2018, a tropical low formed in the Arafura Sea north of the Northern Territory; the Bureau of Meteorology issued a cyclone watch for Darwin, the Tiwi Islands and parts of the north-west Top End.

results.name: Tropical Cyclone Debbie, Queensland, 2017
results.introduction: Tropical Cyclone (TC) Debbie made landfall as a Category 4 Severe Tropical Cyclone near Airlie Beach on 28 March 2017. Storm damage resulted from both the high winds associated with the cyclone, and the very heavy rain. Significant fire and emergency service resources were mobilised.  At least ten fatalities resulted from the flooding and severe weather and hundreds of dwellings were severely damaged.

results.name: Cyclone - Cyclone Marcia, Queensland 2015
results.introduction: In February 2015, severe tropical cyclone Marcia wreaked devastation through Central Queensland. Strong winds, heavy rainfall and floods battered several population centres, including Rockhampton, Biloela, Yeppoon, Byfield, Marmor, Monto, Jambin and parts of the Callide Valley.

results.name: Cyclone Oswald, Queensland & New South Wales 2013
results.introduction: Tropical Cyclone Oswald formed in the Gulf of Carpentaria on 21 January 2013. It made landfall at night between Kowanyama,and Pormpuraaw on the west coast of Cape York Peninsula, as a weak category one system with winds up to 100 km per hour. The following morning, as the cyclone moved inland it was downgraded to a tropical low system. The low moved toward the east coast, near Cooktown on 23 January and then tracked slowly to the south-south-east just inland from the coast, impacting areas near Townsville on 24 January. The weather system stalled over the St. Lawrence-Rockhampton area on the 25th and 26th, and then tracked south to near Dalby west of Brisbane on the 28th, finally accelerating in a southerly direction eventually moving offshore south of Sydney on 29 January.

results.name: Cyclone - Cyclone George, Western Australia
results.introduction: Tropical cyclone George formed on 3 March 2007 in the Joseph Bonaparte Gulf. By the time it crossed the coast, 50 kilometres north-east of Port Hedland on 8 March, it had intensified from a Category 3 to a Category 5 tropical cyclone.
